name: Requirements Traceability
summary: A discipline for linking every requirement to its source, implementation, verification evidence, and change history.
whenToUseIt: Use it when aerospace work must survive certification, audits, supplier handoffs, and late design changes without losing the logic chain.
steps: Capture each requirement with a unique identifier and clear owner.
Link the requirement to its source such as regulation, mission need, or hazard.
Allocate it to subsystems, software items, or drawings.
Link each requirement to a verification method and evidence item.
Maintain the chain whenever a design or requirement changes.
example: A braking-distance requirement may originate from regulation, flow to landing-gear and control-system design, and finally close through test reports. Traceability makes that evidence discoverable when the authority asks for it months later.
